# Man Amongst the Clouds

> A 153,000-word literary fantasy novel by Justin Cronk, published by Stillfire Press.

## What This Book Is

Man Amongst the Clouds is a debut literary fantasy novel in five parts (48 chapters, a prologue, and an epilogue). It tells the story of a boy named Aelo who discovers that magic is memory — that every stone remembers the mountain it was part of, every flame remembers the first spark, and practitioners who learn to listen to the world's remembering pay for it with pieces of who they are.

## The Magic System

Magic in this world is not a force to be wielded. It is a conversation with the world's memory. There are seven disciplines:

- The Know: empathic communion with living things. Cost: your emotional boundaries.
- The Mold: reshaping matter through its memory. Cost: the feeling in your hands.
- The Heal: reminding flesh of wholeness. Cost: you carry every wound you mend.
- The Move: folding distance through spatial memory. Cost: your sense of where you are.
- The Guide: reading the memory of paths and journeys. Cost: your memory of home.
- The Burn: awakening fire from the world's first memory. Cost: the warmth inside you.
- The Sing: harmonizing all seven voices at once. Cost: everything. You dissolve into the memory of the world.

## Key Characters

- Aelo: A fifteen-year-old boy raised on herbs that suppress his ability to hear the world sing. He doesn't know what he is.
- Jalo: The village drunk who burned his own face, broke his own knee, and drugs a child every morning to keep him safe. Every lie is an act of love.
- The Knife (Baara): The king's most lethal weapon. He carries a wooden box of five beautiful objects and checks them every night because the checking is the only act that proves he is still a person.
- King Varas: Born deaf to the Song in a world where everything sings. He has spent seventy years consuming the memories of hundreds to fill a silence nothing can fill.
- Sereth: The Collector of Orphans. She runs a school hidden in the Canopy where children learn to listen to the world's memory.
- Maera: Aelo's mother. Dead before the story begins. Present on every page.

## Structure

The novel is divided into five parts:
- Part I: The Still Water (Prologue + Chapters 1–10) — Available to read free
- Part II: The Waking (Chapters 11–20) — Pre-order available
- Part III: The Breaking (Chapters 21–30)
- Part IV: The Chamber (Chapters 31–40)
- Part V: The Remembering (Chapters 41–48 + Epilogue)

## About the Author

Justin Cronk is a first-time novelist, military veteran, and co-founder of Stillfire Press. He spent nine years writing this book. The magic system is researched from Traditional Chinese Medicine's Five Element Theory, real alchemical processes called spagyrics, herbalism, medieval timber construction, and the world's largest castles. He co-founded Stillfire Press with his son Carter Cronk, who is writing his own debut novel, Ash to Fury.

## Comparable Authors

Readers of Patrick Rothfuss (The Name of the Wind), Robin Hobb (Realm of the Elderlings), Guy Gavriel Kay (Tigana), and Ursula K. Le Guin (A Wizard of Earthsea) will find familiar ground here.

## Keywords

literary fantasy, magic is memory, epic fantasy debut, memory magic system, fantasy novel about sacrifice, books like Name of the Wind, books like Robin Hobb, fantasy books where magic has a cost, indie fantasy novel, self-published fantasy, dark fantasy, debut novel 2026
